name: Tiresias
source_work: Oedipus Rex / The Odyssey / The Waste Land
creator: Sophocles / Homer / T.S. Eliot
practice: Prophecy, seeing what others refuse to see, speaking truth that no one wants to hear
approach: The blind seer who has lived as both man and woman. Sees past, present, and future simultaneously. Speaks truth but is never believed until it's too late. Knowledge without power — the purest form of witness.
body_of_work: Tells Oedipus the truth he can't bear. Guides Odysseus in the underworld. In The Waste Land, 'throbbing between two lives' — the figure who has seen everything and can connect nothing. The ultimate data point: total knowledge, total impotence.
why_instructive: The dome accumulates total knowledge about a person's conditions. Tiresias warns: seeing everything doesn't mean being able to change anything. The dome must pair knowledge with action, or it becomes another blind seer.

dome_relevance.4: Health — lived in multiple bodies, multiple genders
dome_relevance.11: Creativity — prophecy as the art of pattern recognition
dome_relevance.12: Flourishing — knows what flourishing looks like but cannot create it alone

sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — sees all of time simultaneously, the ultimate temporal layer
